Role / Description: This UEI is Northrop Grumman Marine Systems in Sunnyvale. Northrop publicly assigns Sunnyvale work on the Columbia/Dreadnought common-missile-compartment launcher subsystem and also identifies Marine Systems as a supplier of Columbia-class power-generation equipment; the specific subaward line cannot be separated between those product lines from public data.

Subawardee NAICS-6 Description: Power, Distribution, and Specialty Transformer Manufacturing
Role / Description: This UEI is Leonardo DRS’s Menomonee Falls naval-power plant, one of four sites named for Columbia electric-propulsion production. The facility manufactures solid-state propulsion drives and naval power-distribution hardware; DRS does not publicly itemize the exact drive, switchgear, or control unit by UEI.

Manufactured Common Missile Compartment missile-tube assemblies at Rosyth for the U.S. Columbia and U.K. Dreadnought programs. Babcock later transferred these Rosyth operating activities to Rosyth Royal Dockyard Limited.

Scot Forge is a custom forging supplier for marine propulsion and shipbuilding. Its public marine-propulsion materials describe experience producing ship, submarine, and boat shaft forgings, as well as open-die and rolled-ring forgings used as high-strength metal inputs for propulsion and other heavy mechanical applications. Defense-industrial-base coverage also identifies Scot Forge as part of the castings-and-forgings supplier base receiving submarine-industrial-base investment to expand large-forging capacity.

Subawardee NAICS-6 Description: Measuring, Dispensing, and Other Pumping Equipment Manufacturing
Role / Description: Supplies electrical generators for Columbia-class submarines. Curtiss-Wright’s Electro-Mechanical Division received Columbia-specific generator-production contracts valued at approximately $120 million.

Rhoads performs large-scale defense maritime fabrication, assembly, outfitting, welding, pipefitting, and industrial services for Navy submarine construction. Its public materials describe a Philadelphia focus factory for Navy submarine construction, NAVSEA/MIL-spec welding qualifications, and General Dynamics Electric Boat approvals. Rhoads specifically describes moving completed submarine decks and modules from its manufacturing floor to barges bound for Navy test sites or final-assembly shipyard locations.

Precision Custom Components is a custom heavy-fabrication and nuclear-manufacturing supplier. Public company and acquisition materials describe custom-fabricated heavy pressure vessels, reactors, casks, and other heavy-walled components requiring specialized machining, welding, fabrication, engineering, drafting, and in-house manufacturing capability. Public subcontract data show a General Dynamics Electric Boat subaward under the Columbia-class design-completion contract, and local reporting describes PCC as supplying submarine components to the US Navy.
Manufactures missile-tube assemblies and Integrated Tube Hulls for the Common Missile Compartment shared by the Columbia and Dreadnought classes.

Graham supplies mission-critical defense turbomachinery, cryogenic pump, fluid-transfer, power, heat-transfer, and vacuum technology products. For Columbia-class submarines, Graham publicly announced a contract to provide the MK19 Air Turbine Pump assembly. The company also announced shipment of final first-article units for US Navy programs including Columbia, supporting the view that its work is a qualified equipment/subassembly item rather than raw material.

Subawardee NAICS-6 Description: Military Armored Vehicle, Tank, and Tank Component Manufacturing
Role / Description: W International is an advanced metal-fabrication and distributed-shipbuilding supplier for nuclear-submarine structures. Public coverage of HII's acquisition of W International assets describes the Goose Creek, South Carolina facility as producing modules used in Virginia- and Columbia-class submarine construction. HII describes the site as supporting nuclear-powered submarine module and structure construction and producing completed submarine modules and structural units.

CP Industries manufactures high-pressure seamless steel flasks, cylinders, and pressure vessels for military, naval, and aerospace compressed-gas storage. Public materials describe CP as a key supplier of specialized high-pressure vessels to the US Navy for breathing air and shipboard uses, with products integrated into submarine systems for compressed gas storage. Recent Navy procurement data also identify CP Industries as a supplier of air flasks.

APCO publicly supplies submarine assembly and welding fixtures plus specialized handling equipment; shipment records to Electric Boat list J/K fixtures, welding trolleys, hydraulic jacks, caisson-lifting equipment, and related controls. The records reviewed do not explicitly identify which items correspond to its Columbia-class subawards.

Subawardee NAICS-6 Description: Fluid Power Valve and Hose Fitting Manufacturing
Role / Description: Marotta Controls supplies shipboard flow-control and motion-control hardware used on US Navy submarines and surface ships. Public product materials state that every US submarine goes to sea with Marotta ship controls and valves in mission-critical applications, and the company lists high-performance shipboard controls for propulsion, weapons, maneuverability, pressurization, fluid/gas collection and distribution, and other submarine systems. Relevant products include in-line valves, solenoid and motor-operated controls, and manifolds that integrate valves and fluid/gas pathways.

Subawardee NAICS-6 Description: Air-Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment and Commercial and Industrial Refrigeration Equipment Manufacturing
Parent Vendor Name: JOHNSON CONTROLS INTERNATIONAL PUBLIC LIMITED COMPANY
Role / Description: Johnson Controls Navy Systems supplies heating, ventilation, air-conditioning, and refrigeration systems for US Navy vessels, including submarines. Its public Navy/marine materials state that the company designs, manufactures, tests, installs, and supports naval HVAC and refrigeration equipment for platforms ranging from submarines to aircraft carriers. For submarine construction, its role is best characterized as environmental-control/HVAC-R machinery, components, and related engineering/support.

Hunt Valve supplies essential valves and electromechanical actuators for US Navy nuclear submarines. Fairbanks Morse Defense announced purchase orders for Hunt Valve to deliver valves for Virginia- and Columbia-class submarine programs, including Columbia hulls 4 through 7. The public announcement identifies ship service ball valves and forged high-pressure system valves, making Hunt a fluid/pressure-system valve supplier.
Supplies high-integrity steel and stainless-steel castings for the Columbia program. Goodwin confirms U.S. Navy/Columbia work but does not disclose the exact casting or subsystem.

Fabricated large steel structures and modules for Columbia-class submarine construction at its Goose Creek, South Carolina facility. After HII acquired the operation, the same facility and legacy workforce were identified as producing Columbia auxiliary machinery-room and weapons modules.

Subawardee NAICS-6 Description: Turbine and Turbine Generator Set Units Manufacturing
Role / Description: This UEI is DRS Power Technology in Fitchburg, Massachusetts, a Columbia production site specializing in large electric machines and permanent-magnet propulsion technology. It is the DRS site most directly associated with the Columbia-class permanent-magnet main-propulsion motor, although the public contract announcement does not allocate every motor element by UEI.

Pegasus Steel is a nuclear-shipbuilding steel-fabrication supplier for complex structures and assemblies. Public acquisition and KEEL materials identify Pegasus/KEEL work on Columbia, Virginia, and Ford programs, including Columbia-class submarine superstructures and critical complex structures. Its history also references NAVSEA submarine work involving fracture-critical structures and components.

Subawardee NAICS-6 Description: Fluid Power Valve and Hose Fitting Manufacturing
Role / Description: VACCO Industries supplies Columbia-class critical-quiet valves, manifolds, and fluid-control assemblies designed to minimize acoustic signature. VACCO’s own Navy literature lists both Virginia and Columbia among the submarine programs using these products.

The exact cargo billed to this Columbia subaward could not be confirmed. The best-supported role is defense freight forwarding, customs brokerage, and project-cargo logistics for UK-origin Common Missile Compartment hardware and its transportation/storage fixtures moving from Babcock Marine Rosyth to Electric Boat; public manifests identify C. H. Powell/Tandem as the notify/logistics party on such shipments.
Supplies complex hydraulic valves and manifolds for Columbia, including the rudder servovalve/manifold.

Subawardee NAICS-6 Description: Industrial and Commercial Fan and Blower and Air Purification Equipment Manufacturing
Role / Description: Cepeda Associates manufactures, services, and upgrades US Navy submarine life-support and atmosphere-control equipment, including Level I/SUBSAFE-qualified equipment. Its public materials describe submarine systems that remove carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, hydrogen, volatile organic compounds, and related contaminants from the submarine atmosphere. Public Navy-contract notices also identify Cepeda as the original equipment manufacturer for Virginia-class submarine scrubbers and as a repair provider for those scrubbers.

Subawardee NAICS-6 Description: Commercial and Industrial Machinery and Equipment (except Automotive and Electronic) Repair and Maintenance
Role / Description: This appears to be Columbia production infrastructure rather than installed shipboard hardware. Customs records associate Euro Machinery Specialists with delivery/service of large Innse-Berardi horizontal boring and milling centers for Electric Boat; those machines are used at Groton/Quonset to machine large submarine structures and mechanical components, although the specific Columbia part produced on them is not public.

Subawardee NAICS-6 Description: Capacitor, Resistor, Coil, Transformer, and Other Inductor Manufacturing
Role / Description: The exact Columbia unit is not disclosed. The best-supported inference is power-electronic equipment for the submarine’s advanced degaussing/magnetic-signature-control system—converters, current-regulation electronics, or spares that energize hull degaussing coils to cancel the boat’s magnetic field and reduce mine-detection vulnerability.

Advance Mfg. provides precision machining, welded fabrication, large fabrication, and assemblies for defense, marine, power-generation, aerospace, and other high-reliability markets. Public materials list NAVSEA welding specifications, HY-80/HY-100 and related naval materials, large DoD fabrications, and customer certifications that include Electric Boat and Newport News Shipbuilding. Its history notes machined components used on military submarine periscopes, supporting a role as a precision-machined/fabricated component supplier rather than a module prime.

Subawardee NAICS-6 Description: Rubber Product Manufacturing for Mechanical Use
Role / Description: A specific Columbia mount or filter is not publicly named. Oil States’ naval product line and earlier submarine supplier records point to elastomeric acoustic filters, shock mounts, and laminated bearings used under machinery or at piping/duct interfaces in machinery spaces to isolate pumps, motors, compressors, and other equipment from the hull and suppress structure-borne noise.

Subawardee NAICS-6 Description: Fluid Power Cylinder and Actuator Manufacturing
Role / Description: Supplies electrohydraulic linear actuators used on Columbia to operate shipboard valves, throttles, weapons-system mechanisms, doors, and hatches.

Austal USA fabricates and outfits critical module components for the US submarine industrial base. Austal describes a strategic partnership with General Dynamics Electric Boat for fabrication and outfitting of critical components for Virginia- and Columbia-class submarines. Public announcements identify Austal USA's first Columbia-class order as the Command and Control Systems Module for SSBN 828, and also describe work on CCSM and Electronic Deck Module content for submarine programs.

The class-specific valve model and fluid circuit are not public. Portland Valve’s confirmed submarine product is Navy-qualified ship-service ball valves and specialty valves, which would be distributed through Columbia’s seawater, cooling, hydraulic, and other auxiliary piping systems to isolate and control fluid flow.

American Steel and Aluminum is a metal distributor, processor, and contract manufacturer serving defense and shipbuilding customers, including the submarine industrial base. Its public materials describe steel and aluminum sheet, plate, tube, pipe, bar, extrusions, structural shapes, cutting, forming, grinding, deburring, bending, machining, kitting, welding, and supply-chain control. Maine business reporting specifically says its Auburn, Massachusetts operation services General Dynamics Electric Boat's submarine industrial base and that the company cuts and forms plates and other parts for submarines.

A Caterpillar subcontract is publicly reported under Electric Boat’s Columbia-class contract, but the disclosed record does not identify the purchased item. No Columbia-specific onboard component or construction service could be confirmed from public sources.

Subawardee NAICS-6 Description: Measuring, Dispensing, and Other Pumping Equipment Manufacturing
Role / Description: This UEI is CIRCOR Naval Solutions/Warren Pumps in Warren, Massachusetts. It supplies highly engineered centrifugal pumps and pump components for submarine fluid systems; the exact Columbia-class pump service represented by the subaward is not publicly disclosed.

Subawardee Vendor Name: GLOBE COMPOSITE SOLUTIONS, LTD., LIMITED PARTNERSHIP
Role / Description: Globe Composite Solutions designs and manufactures composite and elastomeric components for submarine defense and marine applications, with a focus on acoustic, hydrodynamic, sealing, and mission-critical parts. Public sources identify Globe as a critical supplier of more than 140 parts on Virginia- and Columbia-class submarines, including special hull-treatment-related parts, large-aperture bow acoustic panels, missile hatch gaskets, transducer covers, propulsor fairings, and propeller-shaft snubbers. Globe also describes long-running work designing composite submarine parts and acoustic-signature-reduction components.

Subawardee Vendor Name: L3HARRIS MARITIME POWER & ENERGY SOLUTIONS, INC.
Role / Description: Supplies switchgear and switchboard equipment for the Columbia-class shipboard electrical-distribution system; the program was described as the Ohio-class replacement when the award was announced.

Plainville Electrical Products Company, also branded PEPCO, designs and manufactures electrical switchboards and electrical distribution/control solutions for US Navy platforms, including advanced submarines. Its public materials state that it designs and manufactures electrical switchboards for advanced submarines, destroyers, and surface ships, and provides complete electrical distribution and control solutions. Federal-award profile data also match the company's switchgear and switchboard manufacturing business classification.

Subawardee NAICS-6 Description: Fabricated Pipe and Pipe Fitting Manufacturing
Role / Description: Manufactures Level I high-alloy main-seawater pipe fittings for Columbia. Custom Alloy states it is the sole-source manufacturer approved for Columbia-class Main Seawater Fittings.

This UEI is Curtiss-Wright’s Target Rock operation in East Farmingdale, New York. It manufactures nuclear-qualified valves, actuators, and solenoid assemblies for submarine reactor and fluid systems; the exact Columbia-class valve or service is not publicly identified.

The exact Columbia heat exchanger is not publicly identified. DC Fabricators’ submarine specialty is large steam condensers, air ejectors, and other heat exchangers, making the strongest inference a main condenser/air-ejector or related secondary-steam-plant heat-transfer assembly in the engine room, where turbine exhaust is condensed and heat is rejected to seawater.

Subawardee NAICS-6 Description: Power Boiler and Heat Exchanger Manufacturing
Role / Description: Manufactures naval nuclear-reactor components for Columbia-class submarines. BWXT states that its naval nuclear-propulsion work includes reactor-component production for the Columbia program.

This is non-shipboard digital-engineering support. Beast Code’s Beast Core/IPX tools provide an interactive 2D/3D digital-twin environment that combines geometry, drawings, documents, and metadata for Columbia space arrangement, construction planning, work instructions, and lifecycle support; the exact licensed module or workflow is not disclosed.

Subawardee NAICS-6 Description: All Other Miscellaneous Electrical Equipment and Component Manufacturing
Role / Description: Supplies power-conversion and magnetic components supporting Columbia's electric-drive propulsion system.

Subawardee NAICS-6 Description: Search, Detection, Navigation, Guidance, Aeronautical, and Nautical System and Instrument Manufacturing
Role / Description: A specific Columbia rack or array could not be confirmed. Lockheed Martin’s Liverpool/Syracuse undersea-warfare operation supports the AN/BQQ-10 Acoustic Rapid COTS Insertion common sonar architecture, so the subaward most likely covers sonar signal-processing/interface electronics or software connecting hull arrays to the sonar and combat system in the forward sonar/electronics spaces.
Supplies a sequence of large, high-integrity steel castings for the Columbia program, then called the Ohio replacement program. Public reporting does not disclose the casting geometry or subsystem.

Granite State Manufacturing has publicly said it received a new Columbia product but has not named it. The defensible description is a large, NAVSEA-qualified, precision-machined and welded mission-/life-critical submarine assembly—potentially a structural or equipment-support module—whose exact function and location remain nonpublic.

The exact Columbia bearing is not disclosed. This engineering entity most likely performed design, analysis, and qualification of custom hydrodynamic thrust/journal bearings for a major rotating machine—most plausibly the electric-propulsion train or turbine-generator machinery in the aft engineering spaces—based on Waukesha’s naval bearing line and prior submarine main-thrust-bearing work.

Public sources do not identify the exact Columbia assembly. Craft Machine Works’ relevant capability is final manufacture of large, tight-tolerance machined and complex-welded assemblies, so the subaward most likely covers a complete submarine machinery or structural/equipment-support assembly delivered for installation in a Columbia hull module; its precise location is unconfirmed.
Manufactures composite bow domes for Columbia-class submarines.

A Columbia part number could not be confirmed. B & F Machine’s defense work includes five-axis machining of cases, diffusers, shrouds, rings, linkages, brackets, and mounts; the best-supported inference is precision flow-path or support hardware for a pump/turbomachinery or other mechanical system in the boat’s machinery/auxiliary spaces.

Constructs and expands Electric Boat production facilities used for Columbia manufacturing, including hull-fabrication/advanced-manufacturing buildings and missile-tube production infrastructure. This is shipyard infrastructure, not an onboard component.
Fabricates bearing-support structures and related structural/propulsor demonstration hardware for Columbia's propulsor.
Manufactures Columbia hatches, fairings, hull inserts, and closure doors, and provides SUBSAFE structural fabrication and new-construction support.

Subawardee NAICS-6 Description: Research and Development in the Physical, Engineering, and Life Sciences (except Nanotechnology and Biotechnology)
Role / Description: Supports installation, integration, and testing of Columbia's strategic fire-control system, including mechanical, electrical, and fiber-optic components.

Subawardee NAICS-6 Description: Other Metal Valve and Pipe Fitting Manufacturing
Parent Vendor Name: KME AMERICA MARINE TUBE & FITTING, LLC
Subawardee Vendor Name: KME AMERICA MARINE TUBE & FITTING, LLC
Role / Description: The exact Columbia fitting schedule is not public. KME supplies complex formed elbows, tees, reducers, flanges, and tube fittings in copper-nickel, stainless, and other corrosion-resistant alloys; these parts are installed throughout Columbia’s seawater, cooling, and ship-service piping to route and join pressure-boundary fluid lines.

Subawardee NAICS-6 Description: All Other Miscellaneous Fabricated Metal Product Manufacturing
Role / Description: The evidence points primarily to construction support rather than an installed boat component. Collins & Jewell builds large weldment fixtures, custom submarine support structures, and heavy handling/assembly equipment for Electric Boat’s Groton and Quonset facilities; the specific Columbia fixture or support structure is not publicly named.

The one-time award pattern and company capability indicate Columbia production infrastructure, not shipboard hardware. Ingersoll most likely supplied a custom large-format milling/boring or automated manufacturing system used by Electric Boat to make large submarine structures, tooling, or mechanical components; neither the exact machine nor the Columbia part produced on it is public.

Subawardee NAICS-6 Description: Radio and Television Broadcasting and Wireless Communications Equipment Manufacturing
Role / Description: Confirmed class-specific product family: L3Harris supplies integrated submarine communications-system shipsets for Virginia- and Columbia-class boats. The shipset comprises secure external/satellite and internal communications hardware, software, racks, and interfaces installed in the radio/communications spaces; the exact Camden-produced rack under the earlier subaward is not separately identified.

Curtiss-Wright’s Cheswick electro-mechanical operation produces nuclear-fleet pumps, motors, generators, and propulsion-plant machinery. Public sources do not disclose the exact Columbia-class machine represented by this subaward.
Supplies desurgers and bladder-type surge arrestors used on Columbia fluid systems.

The exact Columbia forging is not publicly named. Lehigh Heavy Forge supplies super-heavy open-die shipbuilding forgings, including very long propulsion shafts and large rings; the subaward therefore most likely covers a high-integrity shaft, ring, or other major forging for the stern propulsion train or another pressure-/load-critical submarine assembly.

The manufacturing entity most likely produced custom hydrodynamic thrust and/or journal-bearing assemblies for one of Columbia’s largest rotating machines—the electric propulsion motor, turbine-generators, or shaft line in the aft engineering spaces. Waukesha is a documented submarine main-thrust-bearing supplier, but the specific Columbia machine and bearing set remain undisclosed.

This is most likely shipyard production equipment rather than an onboard component. Koike supplies CNC plate-cutting systems, welding positioners, turning rolls, and manipulators used to rotate, align, cut, and weld very large hull/module fabrications; the exact Electric Boat workstation and Columbia assembly processed are not public.

The specific Columbia installation versus test use cannot be confirmed. PCB supplies low-noise, internally amplified accelerometers tied in Navy records to Electric Boat drawings; these sensors measure vibration and acoustic signatures on machinery and hull structures during quieting, production acceptance, and sea trials, and may also support onboard condition monitoring.

Subawardee NAICS-6 Description: Iron and Steel Mills and Ferroalloy Manufacturing
Role / Description: The exact Columbia steel grade and plate location are not public. The strongest inference is high-strength heavy plate from the former ArcelorMittal Burns Harbor operation for the pressure hull and other major structural members; that mill supplies military/shipbuilding plate and its steel has previously been documented throughout a Virginia-class submarine hull.
Supplies Level I/SUBSAFE electrical hull penetrators that route transmitter cables through the Columbia pressure hull.

A specific Columbia casting could not be confirmed. The best-supported inference is high-integrity 70/30 copper-nickel or other alloy castings for seawater-system pump/valve bodies, with possible rotating-machinery bearing components; D. W. Clark is an approved Navy casting source and has prior SSBN bearing-casting work.

Confirmed service: Team Submarine selected Sopheon’s Accolade enterprise innovation and new-product-development platform. It supports strategic planning, technology roadmaps, project/portfolio governance, and resource planning across submarine programs including Columbia; it is shore-based program-management software, not equipment installed on the boat.

Subawardee NAICS-6 Description: Commercial and Service Industry Machinery Manufacturing
Role / Description: The UEI maps to Parker’s Village Marine Tec water-purification operation in Carson, California. The best-supported Columbia deliverable is a reverse-osmosis seawater desalination/watermaker package—high-pressure pumps, membranes, controls, and service parts—installed in an auxiliary machinery space to produce potable water; the exact model and capacity are not public.

The strongest class-specific linkage is to the Columbia propulsor forward subassembly. American Tank & Fabricating most likely forms and welds heavy plate components or supporting structures for that pump-jet assembly at the stern, but the public contract data do not expose the exact plate part, drawing, or subassembly element.

Leslie Controls manufactures severe-service regulating, pressure-reducing, and control valves. Its Columbia-class contribution is valve and fluid-control hardware, but public sources do not identify the exact valve set or shipboard service.

This is shipyard-production support, not installed submarine hardware. Industrial Transfer & Storage, now part of MEI, provides heavy rigging, project transport, storage, millwrighting, and precision installation/alignment of large CNC machines and facility equipment at Quonset/Groton; the exact Columbia-production machine moved under the subaward is not disclosed.

No installed Columbia component could be substantiated. Greenland’s documented work is design-build construction of energy and utility infrastructure—HVAC, chilled-water/steam piping, controls, and building retrofits—so the subaward most likely funded a shipyard or mission-critical-facility utility project supporting Columbia production at Electric Boat or Newport News.

Subawardee NAICS-6 Description: Research and Development in the Physical, Engineering, and Life Sciences (except Nanotechnology and Biotechnology)
Role / Description: These subawards sit under Office of Naval Research submarine science-and-technology contracts rather than production hardware buys. The best-supported role is advanced modeling, simulation, sensing/acoustics, networking, or autonomous-payload engineering intended to mature concepts for transition to Virginia and Columbia; the exact BBN work package and any eventual onboard system cannot be confirmed.

Subawardee NAICS-6 Description: All Other Miscellaneous Fabricated Metal Product Manufacturing
Role / Description: The exact Columbia cabinet assignment is not public. Curtiss-Wright/901D supplies shock-, vibration-, and EMI-qualified naval electronics cabinets, enclosures, and operator consoles; these house combat, control, communications, or auxiliary electronics in Columbia’s control/electronics spaces, with Navy notices specifically identifying ACST cabinets from this source.
Provides long-haul truckload transportation and logistics for Columbia construction. The specific cargo carried is not publicly disclosed.

Subawardee NAICS-6 Description: Measuring, Dispensing, and Other Pumping Equipment Manufacturing
Role / Description: This UEI is CIRCOR Pumps North America’s IMO Pump operation in Monroe, North Carolina. It supplies Navy rotary/screw pump-and-motor assemblies and related pump components; the exact Columbia-class fluid-system application is not publicly identified.

A particular Columbia filter model and fluid circuit are not public. Fluid Conditioning Products makes cleanable all-metal mesh/coalescing filter and strainer elements qualified for Navy SUBSAFE Level I service, making the likely deliverable pressure-boundary filtration for seawater, hydraulic, lubricating-oil, fuel, or another critical machinery-space fluid system.

Parent Vendor Name: EMERSON AUTOMATION SOLUTIONS FINAL CONTROL US LP
Subawardee Vendor Name: EMERSON AUTOMATION SOLUTIONS FINAL CONTROL US LP
Role / Description: The exact Columbia valve tag and circuit are not publicly disclosed. Emerson’s Mansfield Navy product operation supplies pressure-/safety-relief, control, and angle valves; these protect submarine piping and equipment from overpressure and regulate flow in machinery and auxiliary systems.

Provides large-scale precision welding, fabrication, and machining of heavy metal components for Virginia- and Columbia-class submarine programs. Public sources do not disclose the exact Columbia part.

Subawardee NAICS-6 Description: Fabricated Pipe and Pipe Fitting Manufacturing
Role / Description: Manufactures specialized alloy forgings, custom fittings, and pipe for naval and defense applications. As successor to Custom Alloy's High Bridge operation, it continues the fitting/forging business; public sources do not separately identify which post-acquisition Columbia fitting each subaward covers.
Develops and administers workforce training and learning content for Electric Boat personnel building Virginia- and Columbia-class submarines.

Public sources confirm that Fairlead supplies shipboard integrated parts for Virginia- and Columbia-class submarines but do not name this assembly. Based on its nuclear-shipbuilding capabilities, the best-supported inference is a precision-fabricated/machined mechanical subassembly, machinery support/skid, or integrated power/control structure installed in a Columbia machinery or electrical module; the exact item remains unconfirmed.

The best public evidence identifies PRL as a Columbia supplier of critical cast pump and valve components. PRL pours, heat-treats, machines, and nondestructively tests high-integrity bodies and other cast parts used in the boat’s seawater, cooling, hydraulic, and other machinery/auxiliary fluid systems; the exact pump/valve model is not public.

Other Measuring and Controlling Device Manufacturing
Rubber Product Manufacturing for Mechanical Use

Radio and Television Broadcasting and Wireless Communications Equipment Manufacturing
Role / Description: The Fort Walton Beach DRS Training & Control Systems entity develops training, control, and wireless-communications electronics. I found no public source identifying a distinct Columbia-class construction component supplied by this UEI.

Role / Description: DY4/Curtiss-Wright Defense Solutions supplies rugged VPX embedded computers and processing modules. The entity’s products are suitable for submarine control and mission systems, but the exact Columbia-class subsystem represented by this subaward is not publicly identified.
